title: Berkheya eriobasis
intro: Berkheya eriobasis is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, commonly known as a daisy-like herb. Traditionally, Berkheya eriobasis has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Berkheya eriobasis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Berkheya eriobasis is typically prepared as a tea or infused in oil to take advantage of its health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Berkheya eriobasis is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants that are primarily known for producing showy flowers and seeds. The genus Berkheya itself is a group of flowering plants native to southern Africa. The family Asteraceae is further divided into various subfamilies, tribes, and genera. Berkheya eriobasis is a specific species within this broader classification.

It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
